function.name: board_clear
function.description: Clear blackboard entries for a topic. Two modes — soft (default) renames the topic to '[cleared] <topic>' so the rows stay around (audit-preserving, idempotent). Hard (pass {"yes":true}) DELETEs all rows with that topic (cannot be undone — there's no board-level audit log). For a true wipe of EVERYTHING on the blackboard regardless of topic, use `rm -f .data/blackboard.db`.

topic.description: The topic to clear (must match exactly — wildcards not supported; use multiple calls for prefix-based clearing)

yes.description: If true, perform a HARD full-wipe (DELETE all rows for that topic). Default false = soft rename to '[cleared] <topic>' (rows preserved, audit trail preserved).
